name: Vaex (Out-of-Core DataFrames)

performance.notes: Memory-mapped I/O; visualizes/aggregates billion-row datasets without loading into RAM

agent_readiness.notes: Out-of-core DataFrame library for billion-row datasets. Memory-mapped files (HDF5/Arrow) enable working with datasets larger than RAM. Lazy evaluation. Fast groupby and aggregations. Visualization for big data (2D histograms, heatmaps). Machine learning feature engineering at scale. Pandas-like API. Slower development recently — Polars and DuckDB cover most use cases better now. Still useful for billion-row visualization workflows.

gotchas: Slower development pace — some features behind DuckDB/Polars
Less community activity in recent years
Polars/DuckDB often better for most use cases
best_when: Visualizing billion-row datasets that don't fit in RAM
avoid_when: When Polars or Dask meets the need
summary: Vaex is an out-of-core DataFrame library for billion-row datasets that don't fit in RAM. Memory-mapped files, lazy evaluation, fast groupby/aggregation, and big data visualization. Pandas-like API. Slower development pace recently — DuckDB and Polars cover most of its use cases better. Still useful for billion-row visualization workflows without cluster computing.
